reason: Both answers correctly recommend talking the team out of full event sourcing, propose an append-only ledger plus boring CRUD as the substitute, flag event/schema versioning and re-rating-under-new-rules as the key danger, and recommend evaluating existing billing providers (Stripe/Lago/Metronome) before building. Both are similarly concise and well-organized. A adds one extra concrete practical detail (idempotent usage ingestion with dedupe keys) and a clear scoping fallback if the team insists on ES. B adds a "when I'd talk you into it" section and opens by implicitly naming the pattern-vs-problem enthusiasm trap ("audit trail! replay! immutability!"), which more directly gestures at the red-flag key point. Neither spells out that red flag explicitly and fully, but B edges slightly closer. Overall the two answers are close enough in correctness and usefulness that neither clearly outperforms the other.
